compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
IMPLEMENTED + PUSHED (2026-07-18). Gerrit change #798 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/798), commit e91cdd24f, Change-Id I4c42778f1c4b5777be876a6677c50d421449a8a8. Plan-review PASSED + signed (head 5ce636f21) before claim.

DELIVERED: criterion_effectiveness.py (recorder --record[--backfill] + --report), committed metrics runs/criterion_effectiveness.json over the existing corpus (40 criteria auto-included; 17 non-null detection_proxy 0.74-0.9; 12 blocking_fp_proxy>0), tests/unit/test_criterion_effectiveness.py (11 tests), docs (plan-review-gate.md promotion-gate protocol + experiments README). All ACs proven THIS SESSION over the existing ~37k-firing corpus; deterministic, ZERO LLM calls.

DEVIATION (recorded per transparency): AC1/Scope described committing runs/criterion_firings.jsonl. DISCOVERED the pre-commit check-added-large-files gate caps NEW files at 500 KB; the full firing ledger is ~8 MB over the current corpus. RESOLUTION: the ledger is now GIT-IGNORED as a local, regenerable standing artifact (analogous to .tickets-tracker/), and the small computed metrics artifact runs/criterion_effectiveness.json is the committed CI-visible baseline. AC1/AC2/AC3 (recorder writes >5000-row ledger, idempotent, --report) remain MET as this-session live proofs (like R7's --verify-repro); the ledger-reproduces-committed-metrics drift check is a local test (skipped in CI). Docs + .gitignore updated to match. This is an empirical-shape refinement, not a scope reduction.

NEXT: awaiting LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1, then Submit, then close.

compiled_state.description:
## Context / problem

R7 (child `b1a4`) shipped `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/gate_eval_instrumentation.py`,
which computes per-criterion `blocking_fp_proxy` + `advisory_application_rate` by joining each
reviewed ticket's **outcome-corpus** row (post-claim edit class, reopen count, force-close â€” mined
from the `tickets` orphan branch's git objects by `mine_outcome_corpus.py`) to the ticket's
`REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar findings. That join is the source of its signal and its cost: it needs a
full git-object walk of the tickets branch per refresh, so it is a periodic batch job, not a cheap
standing recorder, and its FP signal is the post-hoc force-close/reopen fact.

**Beneficiary + problem.** The epic's own dogfood-monitoring plan (this epic's success criteria and
`docs/plan-review-gate.md` "Blocking-FP-proxy demotion") says new criteria enter at **advisory**
posture and are watched for promotion/demotion via per-criterion dogfood metrics. As R1/R3/R4 ship
new advisory criteria, the gate maintainer needs those criteria monitored **automatically, at zero
marginal LLM cost, without per-criterion wiring**, and needs a *detection* signal (does the
criterion catch real, acted-on defects?) that R7 does not provide. Today there is no standing,
sidecar-native recorder that accumulates per-criterion firing history and computes a
detection/blocking-FP proxy without the outcome-corpus git-walk.

## What this delivers (DISTINCT from and complementary to R7 â€” not a duplicate)

A **standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder** whose signal source is the sidecar
**re-review history ALONE** â€” no outcome corpus, no git-object walk, so it is cheap and incremental.
Concretely:

1. **Recorder** â€” a new harness `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/criterion_effectiveness.py`
   that reads the persisted `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars and appends one lean **firing row per
   (review-round, finding)** into an append-only, **prune-immune** ledger
   `runs/criterion_firings.jsonl`. Each row uses **short keys** (compact + jq-friendly), documented
   in the harness docstring: `t`=ticket_id, `ts`=review_ts_ns (int), `r`=round_uuid, `v`=verdict,
   `c`=criteria (list), `n`=norm_id, `u`=fix_unit_key, `d`=decision, `s`=severity, `p`=priority,
   `x`=drop_reason. **Where the envelope identity comes from:** the review's `ts_ns` and `round_uuid`
   are parsed from the sidecar **filename** (`<ts_ns>-<uuid>-REVIEW_RESULT.json`, the format
   `event_append` writes and that `sidecar.all_review_results` orders by) â€” the recorder mirrors
   `all_review_results`'s file-enumeration + v1/v2 schema guard but reads the filename directly,
   because `all_review_results` returns only the payload `data` body and drops the event envelope
   (so ts/uuid are NOT available from it). The per-finding fields (`c`/`n`/`d`/`s`/`p`/`x`) come from
   the payload's `findings[]`; `u`=`fix_unit_key` is computed via the production
   `sidecar.fix_unit_key`. It **auto-includes any criterion id it sees** (no per-criterion
   registration). `--backfill` seeds the ledger from the entire existing corpus; the default
   `--record` mode is incremental (appends only reviews whose filename `ts_ns` exceeds the ledger's
   max recorded `ts`), and re-running EITHER mode is **idempotent** (dedup key
   `(t, ts, r, n)` = ticket + review_ts + round_uuid + norm_id â€” so `--backfill` is also
   re-runnable without duplication). The ledger is prune-immune because the sidecar's own retention
   prune caps history at `RETAIN_PER_TICKET=50` rounds/ticket â€” a durable firing ledger captures
   firings before that prune, which is the standing recorder's reason to exist.

2. **`--report`** â€” computes, per criterion over a trailing `--window` (the N most-recently-reviewed
   tickets, ranked by each ticket's max `review_ts`; windowing per-ticket keeps each ticket's full
   round history intact, which the disposition logic below needs), from the ledger ALONE:
   - `detection_proxy` = fraction of the criterion's distinct blocking fix-units (keyed by
     `fix_unit_key`, criteria-free, attributed to the criterion via the firing's `c` criteria list)
     that the ticket later **remediated to a PASS** â€” i.e. the fix-unit is a blocking firing in some
     round and is **absent from the ticket's latest round AND that latest round's verdict is PASS**.
     A latest round whose verdict is `BLOCK` or `INDETERMINATE` is treated as **non-PASS**
     (conservative: not counted as remediated). Denominator = "resolvable" blocking fix-units (a
     round exists after the fix-unit's first blocking appearance, so a disposition is observable).
     This is a caught-then-fixed "true-positive-ish" proxy R7 does not compute at all.
   - `blocking_fp_proxy` = fraction of the criterion's distinct blocking fix-units the gate later
     **de-escalated** (in a round after its first blocking appearance the same fix-unit is found but
     no longer surfaced as blocking â€” decision `dropped`) and never remediated to a PASS. The gate's
     own re-review reversed the earlier block, a conservative within-review FP lower bound. The
     `dropped` decision deliberately subsumes BOTH a Pass-3 threshold drop and the novelty/completion
     convergence floors: an empirical check of the existing corpus shows the floors are inert by
     default (every `dropped` finding carries `drop_reason` null), so a floor-only signal would be
     structurally dead â€” keying off `decision == "dropped"` is what gives the proxy real signal now
     (the per-firing `drop_reason` is still recorded to LABEL floor-driven drops for future floor-era
     segmentation). This is a **deliberately DIFFERENT metric from R7's** same-named proxy: R7 counts
     blocking findings whose ticket was later force-closed/reopened (an outcome-corpus, post-hoc
     signal); this counts blocking fix-units the gate reversed across re-review rounds (a
     sidecar-only, within-review signal). The two are complementary FP lenses with different base
     rates, not a redefinition of R7's â€” R7's harness and metric are untouched, and R7's 10% alarm
     threshold is calibrated to ITS force-close proxy, not transferable as-is to this one.
   - `sample_counts` = the denominators (distinct blocking fix-units, resolvable fix-units, advisory
     firings, distinct tickets, total firings) so a rate is `null` (not fabricated) when its
     denominator is 0.
   `--report` writes `runs/criterion_effectiveness.json` and auto-includes **every** criterion id in
   the ledger. **`--report` reads the committed ledger as-is and never auto-records**; the
   `--no-refresh` flag is an explicit alias for that default (kept for symmetry with R7's
   `--no-refresh` and to make AC3's proving command self-documenting), so the report is deterministic
   over the committed artifact whether or not `--no-refresh` is passed. To refresh first, run
   `--record` then `--report` as two steps.

3. **Wiring / cadence.** The standing invocation is documented (a periodic `--record` incremental
   append, session-log-style, then `--report`), following R7's precedent (a harness under
   `docs/experiments/` + a thin CI unit test of its pure functions), and `docs/plan-review-gate.md`
   records the production emit-time hook (`sidecar.emit` in
   `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py`) as the future integration seam. This deliberately does
   NOT modify the review hot path, keeping the verdict byte-identical and the emit best-effort-safe.

**Approach rationale (why this shape, alternatives weighed).** A documented harness + standing
`--record` was chosen over a production emit-hook because it keeps the review hot path byte-identical
and best-effort-safe, matches the R7 precedent (so the two dogfood jobs read alike), avoids growing
any module-size-pinned production file, and the append-only ledger is prune-immune where reading
sidecars live at report time is not (the 50-round retention prune drops older firings). The
sidecar-history-only signal was chosen over re-using R7's outcome corpus specifically to make the
recorder standing and $0 â€” reusing that corpus would re-introduce the git-walk cost and duplicate
R7's metric rather than complement it. This is deterministic instrumentation over data that already
exists: **no LLM call, no batch eval, no new eval spend.**

## Scope â€” files

- NEW `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/criterion_effectiveness.py` â€” recorder + report
  (target 200â€“500 LOC; a docs/experiments harness, so not collected by pytest and not scanned by
  the src/rebar module-size gate).
- NEW `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/criterion_firings.jsonl` â€” the append-only firing
  ledger, backfilled from the existing corpus (committed; CI-visible, since `.tickets-tracker/` is
  git-ignored and absent in CI).
- NEW `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/criterion_effectiveness.json` â€” the metrics artifact
  over the EXISTING corpus (committed proof it works now).
- NEW `tests/unit/test_criterion_effectiveness.py` â€” deterministic unit tests of the pure functions
  (loads the harness by path, exactly as `tests/unit/test_gate_eval_classifier.py` loads R7's).
- EDIT `docs/plan-review-gate.md` â€” add the standing-recorder + advisoryâ†’blocking promotion-gate
  protocol (the section R1/R3/R4 reference), stating the R7 distinction.
- EDIT `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md` â€” usage under the existing "Gate-eval
  instrumentation (R7 â€¦)" material.

**Reused existing symbols (verified present).** `rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.all_review_results`,
`sidecar.fix_unit_key`, `sidecar.norm_id`, `sidecar.EVENT_TYPE`, `sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET` (all in
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py`); the R7 harness `gate_eval_instrumentation.py` and its test
`tests/unit/test_gate_eval_classifier.py` (the path-load pattern). Firing fingerprints are computed
via the production `sidecar.fix_unit_key` / stored `norm_id` â€” this recorder does NOT re-implement
fingerprint logic.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] **AC1 (recorder + auto-inclusion).** `criterion_effectiveness.py --record --backfill` reads
  the live sidecar corpus and writes `runs/criterion_firings.jsonl`, one JSON row per
  (review-round, finding), auto-including every criterion id present. PROVE:
  `python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/criterion_effectiveness.py --record --backfill`
  then `test $(wc -l < docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/criterion_firings.jsonl) -gt 5000` and
  `test $(jq -r '.c[]' docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/criterion_firings.jsonl | sort -u | wc -l) -ge 30`.
- [ ] **AC2 (idempotent incremental append).** Re-running the recorder appends no duplicate firings
  (dedup key `(ticket, review_ts, round_uuid, norm_id)`). PROVE:
  `N=$(wc -l < â€¦/criterion_firings.jsonl); python â€¦/criterion_effectiveness.py --record --backfill;
  test $(wc -l < â€¦/criterion_firings.jsonl) -eq "$N"`.
- [ ] **AC3 (`--report` over trailing window, auto-includes every criterion).**
  `criterion_effectiveness.py --report --no-refresh --window 400` reads the committed ledger and
  writes `runs/criterion_effectiveness.json` with, per criterion, `detection_proxy`,
  `blocking_fp_proxy`, and `sample_counts` (integer denominators). PROVE: run it, then
  `test $(jq 'keys|length' â€¦/criterion_effectiveness.json) -ge 30` and
  `jq -e 'all(.[]; has("detection_proxy") and has("blocking_fp_proxy") and (.sample_counts.blocking_fix_units|type=="number"))' â€¦/criterion_effectiveness.json`.
- [ ] **AC4 (deterministic pure functions, CI-tested).** `tests/unit/test_criterion_effectiveness.py`
  asserts, on synthetic fixtures: (a) `detection_proxy` counts a blocking fix-unit remediated to a
  later PASS round; (b) `blocking_fp_proxy` counts a blocking fix-unit later floor-suppressed
  (`drop_reason` set) and never remediated; (c) a criterion id unseen by any prior fixture is
  auto-included in the report; (d) `--window` bounds the tickets considered; (e) a criterion with a
  zero denominator yields `null` (not a fabricated `0.0`) for that proxy. PROVE:
  `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_criterion_effectiveness.py -q` exits 0.
- [ ] **AC5 (works over EXISTING data NOW, not future data).** The committed
  `runs/criterion_effectiveness.json` is computed over the existing sidecar corpus and carries real
  values: at least one criterion has a non-null `detection_proxy` AND at least one has a non-null
  `blocking_fp_proxy`. PROVE:
  `test $(jq '[.[]|select(.detection_proxy!=null)]|length' â€¦/criterion_effectiveness.json) -ge 1` and
  `test $(jq '[.[]|select(.blocking_fp_proxy!=null)]|length' â€¦/criterion_effectiveness.json) -ge 1`.
- [ ] **AC6 (docs: standing cadence + promotion-gate protocol + R7 distinction).**
  `docs/plan-review-gate.md` gains a section documenting the standing recorder, the
  advisoryâ†’blocking promotion gate that R1/R3/R4 reference (auto-inclusion = zero per-criterion
  wiring), and how this recorder is distinct from R7's outcome-corpus metric; the experiments README
  documents the `--record`/`--report` usage. PROVE:
  `grep -q "criterion_effectiveness" docs/plan-review-gate.md && grep -q "promotion" docs/plan-review-gate.md`
  and `grep -q "criterion_effectiveness" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/README.md`.
- [ ] **AC7 (zero-LLM + gates green).** The recorder makes no LLM/agent call (deterministic only),
  and the change passes lint, typecheck, and the module-size gate with no pinned file grown. PROVE:
  `! grep -iE "anthropic|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY|invoke_(model|agent)|runner\\.run" docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/criterion_effectiveness.py`
  and `make lint` and `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q` exit 0.

## Testing plan

Deterministic unit tests only (no LLM). The heavy detection/FP/window logic is proven on small
in-memory fixtures with known round sequences (RED-then-GREEN for each rule); a lightweight
committed-corpus assertion proves non-triviality over real data. The `--record`/`--report` IO paths
are exercised by the AC proving commands this session against the existing corpus (the recorder's
live-store read mirrors R7's `--verify-repro` manual-verification posture; the committed ledger is
the CI-visible artifact, since `.tickets-tracker/` is git-ignored and absent in CI).

## Sequencing / dependencies

No file overlap with R1â€“R6 (this adds a NEW harness, NEW runs artifacts, a NEW test, and appends
doc sections). It complements R7 (`b1a4`, already landed) and is referenced by R1/R3/R4's advisory
criteria via auto-inclusion. Lands through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1), then closes.

file_impact.reason: append-only firing ledger backfilled from existing corpus
per-criterion metrics artifact over existing data
deterministic unit tests of pure functions

compiled_state.title: Standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder (sidecar-native dogfood detection/blocking-FP proxies, zero-LLM)
